pattern: Counting the descents and greedily choosing whether to lower the current value or raise the next one decides if a single modification can make the array non-decreasing.
hints: At most one change is allowed, so more than one violation makes it impossible.
When you hit nums[i] > nums[i+1], you must fix it, and how you fix it matters for the next comparison.
If lowering nums[i+1] is unsafe (because nums[i-1] already exceeds it) raise nums[i] instead by setting nums[i+1] = nums[i]; allow only one such repair.

edge_inputs.description: lowering the next element is unsafe so raise instead
drop at the front can be fixed by lowering first

stress.description: random arrays small in range so descents are common
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(10000, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
